summary: First-pass testing for Le Jean Michel Breizh restaurant site. Backend (pytest) and frontend (Playwright) both pass end-to-end. 8/8 backend tests pass: GET /api/restaurant, POST/GET /api/reservations (with validation on email/guests/name), POST/GET /api/contact (with validation on email/message). MongoDB _id correctly excluded from list responses. Frontend: homepage loads, navbar links and Reserver button work, reservation form (date popover with Mon/Sun disabled, time select, guests select, name/email/phone/notes) submits successfully and shows the success card with correct data; contact form submits and shows the success toast then clears.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown') — consider FastAPI lifespan handlers for forward-compat.
Reservation/Contact POST returns 200 instead of 201 Created — acceptable but 201 would be more REST-correct.
No rate-limiting on public POST endpoints (reservations, contact); easy to abuse since the site is public. Consider basic throttling or CAPTCHA before production.

Reservation date/time stored as free-form strings (no validation that date is in the future or that time is within opening hours) — frontend enforces it, but backend should also validate to prevent bad data via direct API calls.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Test entries created with TEST_ prefix in `reservations` and `contact_messages` collections (id-based, not destructive).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py covers all 4 routes + validation. Reuse and extend as endpoints grow. Calendar disables Sun (0) and Mon (1); for Playwright pick a future Tue–Sat. data-testids are stable across components.
